arts.ttl: Emiliano Martinez makes new Aston Villa demand after admitting cup frustrations
arts.sum: Goalkeeper Emi Martinez has tasted World Cup glory with his country Argentina, but he admits he is desperate to end Aston Villa's trophy drought in the FA Cup this season

arts.ttl: Aston Villa: The Art of Legacy
arts.sum: Aston Villa further celebrated the club’s 150th anniversary with a special exhibition at the Birmingham Museum & Art Gallery on Thursday night. The limited edition event showcased the inspiration behind our landmark anniversary kit, blending art, history and football culture into an unforgettable experience to mark Villa’s historic milestone. Recently reopened following extensive renovation, the spectacular Birmingham Museum & Art Gallery hosted around 150 guests for an evening of creativity and celebration of all things claret and blue. The exhibition brought the artistry of Villa’s legacy to life, showcasing the deep connection between the club’s history and its bold, modern vision. The evening featured live art creation along with curated photography highlighting special moments from the kit shoot, all brought to life through immersive displays. Audio-visual storytelling complemented dynamic content that narrated the evolution of the club and its kit, along with historic memorabilia curated in collaboration with historians, celebrating Villa’s rich heritage. President of Football Operations Monchi, Director of Football Operations Damian Vidagany and President of Business Operations Chris Heck were present at the celebration. And they were joined by a host of former players and club legends, including Peter McParland (pictured), Tony Morley, Kenny Swain, Pat Heard, Brian Little, Ian Taylor, Alan McInally, Stiliyan Petrov, Thomas Hitzlsperger and many more. The exhibition was accompanied by live music from lifelong Villa supporter and DJ Nathan Dawe, bringing a soundtrack for this landmark celebration in an iconic setting.

arts.ttl: West Ham join race to sign Man United forward Marcus Rashford but face cost hurdle - as new boss Graham Potter seeks reinforcements amid absences in attack
arts.sum: West Ham are currently without captain and top scorer Jarrod Bowen, who is expected to be out for another five weeks with a fractured foot, while Michail Antonio is ruled out for the rest of the season.

arts.ttl: David Moyes: Everton owners set date to appoint new manager after Sean Dyche sacking?
arts.sum: Everton's new owners - the Friedkin Group - reportedly set a date for when they wish to appoint a new manager, with ex-Toffees boss David Moyes emerging as their top target to replace Sean Dyche.

arts.ttl: Everton 2-0 Peterborough: Leighton Baines issues Armando Broja injury update, reacts to Sean Dyche sacking, David Moyes rumours
arts.sum: Leighton Baines reflects on a "difficult day" that ended on a high with Everton beating Peterborough United 2-0 in the FA Cup third round on Thursday night, just hours after the sacking of Sean Dyche.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery responds to Diego Carlos speculation after Milan Skriniar transfer twist
arts.sum: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has responded to speculation surrounding Diego Carlos, who is a target for Fenerbahce this month. Last week, Emery was asked about Carlos and his stance on a possible sale, but he spoke of the defender's importance in the side considering the lack of options in that department of his squad.

arts.ttl: David Moyes can be left in no doubt about tough job he's walking into on return to Everton
arts.sum: EVERTON 2-0 PETERBOROUGH: On the day that Everton sacked Sean Dyche and moved to the brink of appointing David Moyes, their problems were there for all to see

arts.ttl: Ipswich agree £20m deal for Villa winger Philogene
arts.sum: Aston Villa winger Jaden Philogene is set for a medical at Ipswich Town before a £20m move to the club.

arts.ttl: David Moyes poised for a dream return to Premier League management; talks ongoing
arts.sum: Everton have begun their search for a new manager following the sacking of Sean Dyche, with former Toffees boss David Moyes reportedly emerging as the frontrunner for the role. The club’s new owners, The Friedkin Group (TFG), officially parted ways with Dyche after a disappointing two-year tenure at Goodison Park. arts.ttl: Why West Ham and why now? Is Potter the right fit?
arts.sum: BBC Sport examines whether new West Ham boss Graham Potter is a good fit for the job.
